detaileddescription.para: signal about xf changing triggered in setXf and setWorldXf, it is called for children too triggered in addChild and addChildBefore, it is called only for children object

briefdescription.para: Prefer to not read directly. Use the getter, as it can be overridden.
finds a direct child by name

detaileddescription.para:
this space to parent space transformation (to world space if no parent) for default or given viewport
isDef
receives true if the object has default transformation in this viewport (same as xf() returns)

briefdescription.para: forgets specific transform in given viewport (or forgets all specific transforms for {} input)
returns xfs for all viewports, combined into a single object
modifies xfs for all viewports at once

detaileddescription.para:
this space to world space transformation for default or specific viewport
isDef
receives true if the object has default transformation in this viewport (same as worldXf() returns)

briefdescription.para: scale object size (all point positions)
returns all viewports where this object is visible together with all its parents
returns true if this object is visible together with all its parents in any of given viewports
if true sets all predecessors visible, otherwise sets this object invisible
object properties lock for UI

detaileddescription.para: If true, the scene tree GUI doesn't allow you to drag'n'drop this object into a different parent. Defaults to false.

briefdescription.para: returns parent object in the tree
return true if given object is ancestor of this one, false otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Find a common ancestor between this object and the other one. Returns null on failure (which is impossible if both are children of the scene root). Will return this if other matches this.
removes this from its parent children list returns false if it was already orphan

briefdescription.para: an object can hold other sub-objects

memberdef.definition: const std::vector< std::shared_ptr< const Object > > & MR::Object::children
memberdef.name: children

detaileddescription.para: adds given object in the recognized children before existingChild; if newChild was already among this children then moves it just before existingChild keeping the order of other children intact; returns false if newChild is nullptr, or existingChild is not a child of this

briefdescription.para: returns false if it was not child of this
detaches all recognized children from this, keeping all unrecognized ones
sort recognized children by name
selects the object, returns true if value changed, otherwise returns false

detaileddescription.para: ancillary object is an object hidden (in scene menu) from a regular user such objects cannot be selected, and if it has been selected, it is unselected when turn ancillary

briefdescription.para: returns true if the object or any of its ancestors are ancillary
sets the object visible in the viewports specified by the mask (by default in all viewports)
checks whether the object is visible in any of the viewports specified by the mask (by default in any viewport)
specifies object visibility as bitmask of viewports
gets object visibility as bitmask of viewports
this method virtual because others data model types could have dirty flags or something
clones all tree of this object (except ancillary and unrecognized children)
clones current object only, without parent and/or children

detaileddescription.para: clones all tree of this object (except ancillary and unrecognied children) clones only pointers to mesh, points or voxels
clones current object only, without parent and/or children clones only pointers to mesh, points or voxels

briefdescription.para: return several info lines that can better describe object in the UI

memberdef.type: MRMESH_API MR_BIND_IGNORE Expected< std::vector< std::future< Expected< void > > > >
memberdef.definition: MRMESH_API MR_BIND_IGNORE Expected< std::vector< std::future< Expected< void > > > > MR::Object::serializeRecursive
memberdef.name: serializeRecursive

detaileddescription.para:
creates futures that save this object subtree: models in the folder by given path and fields in given JSON
childId
is its ordinal number within the parent This would be automatically skipped in the bindings anyway because of the Json::Value parameter. But skipping it here prevents the vector-of-futures type from being registered, which is helpful. TODO: figure out how to automate this (add a flag to the parser to outright reject functions based on their parameter and return types).

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::filesystem::path &path, const Json::Value &root, ProgressCallback progressCb={}, int *objCounter=nullptr)
memberdef.name: deserializeRecursive

detaileddescription.para: loads subtree into this Object models from the folder by given path and fields from given JSON
swaps this object with other note: do not swap object signals, so listeners will get notifications from swapped object requires implementation of swapBase_ and swapSignals_ (if type has signals)

briefdescription.para: returns bounding box of this object in world coordinates for default or specific viewport
returns bounding box of this object and all children visible in given (or default) viewport in world coordinates

detaileddescription.para: empty box

briefdescription.para: does the object have any visual representation (visible points, triangles, edges, etc.), no considering child objects

detaileddescription.para: does the object have any model available (but possibly empty), e.g. ObjectMesh has valid mesh() or ObjectPoints has valid pointCloud()
provides read-only access to the tag storage the storage is a set of unique strings
adds tag to the object's tag storage additionally calls ObjectTagManager::tagAddedSignal NOTE: tags starting with a dot are considered as service ones and might be hidden from UI
removes tag from the object's tag storage additionally calls ObjectTagManager::tagRemovedSignal

briefdescription.para: returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
return true if model of current object equals to model (the same) of other
return hash of model (or hash object pointer if object has no model)

simplesect.para: this ctor is public only for std::make_shared used inside clone()

briefdescription.para: return name of subtype for serialization purposes
return human readable name of subclass
return human readable name of subclass in plural form
user should not be able to call copy implicitly, use clone() function instead
swaps whole object (signals too)

detaileddescription.para:
swaps signals, used in swap function to return back signals after swapBase_
pls call Parent::swapSignals_ first when overriding this function

memberdef.definition: virtual MRMESH_API Expected< std::future< Expected< void > > > MR::Object::serializeModel_
memberdef.name: serializeModel_

detaileddescription.para: Creates future to save object model (e.g. mesh) in given file path is full filename without extension

Write parameters to given Json::Value,
if you override this method, please call Base::serializeFields_(root) in the beginning

briefdescription.para: Reads model from file.
shares model from other object

detaileddescription.para:
Reads parameters from json value
if you override this method, please call Base::deserializeFields_(root) in the beginning

This calls onWorldXfChanged_() for all children recursively, which in turn emits worldXfChangedSignal. This isn't virtual because it wouldn't be very useful, because it doesn't call itself on the children (it doesn't use a true recursion, instead imitating one, presumably to save stack space, though this is unlikely to be an issue).

briefdescription.para: Emits worldXfChangedSignal, but derived classes can add additional behavior to it.

memberdef.definition: Expected< std::vector< std::future< Expected< void > > > > MR::Object::serializeRecursive_
memberdef.argsstring: (const std::filesystem::path &path, Json::Value &root, int childId, MapSharedObjects *mapSharedObjects) const
memberdef.name: serializeRecursive_

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::filesystem::path &path, const Json::Value &root, int *objCounter, MapLinkToSharedObjectModel &mapLinkToSharedObjectModel, const ProgressCallback &progressCb)
memberdef.name: deserializeRecursive_

briefdescription.para: named object in the data model
